title: Implement a query-string parser
prompt: Create `qs.mjs` exporting `parseQuery(str)` that parses a URL query string (without the leading '?') into an object. Split on '&', then on the first '='. URL-decode both keys and values. A key with no '=' maps to an empty string. Example: parseQuery('a=1&b=hello%20world&c') === { a: '1', b: 'hello world', c: '' }.
